children.name: Air emissions
children.description: Refers to non-greenhouse gas air emissions that impact air quality, atmospheric conditions and/or human health. These may be emitted through company operations or through the use of company products, most commonly fossil fuel combustion engines. Air quality references may be related to indoor air quality, e.g. from VOCs, or outdoor air quality.

children.name: Alternative fuels
children.description: Refers to fuels that, when combusted, have a lower climate impact than traditional fossil fuels and are aimed at supporting a low-carbon transition

children.name: Ambient pollution
children.description: Refers to sensory exposure to external factors that are harmful or unpleasant in the immediate surroundings. The effects can range from health problems to ecological imbalances. The topic also mentions initiatives and solutions to reduce the exposure or its consequences.

children.name: Biodiversity
children.description: Refers to the impacts on or preservation of the genetic and species varieties of flora and fauna present within a biome(s), including impacts of fishing. It covers references to ecosystem services - the various benefits provided to humans by a healthy ecosystem

children.name: Drug resistance & pharmaceuticals in the environment
children.description: Refers to pharmaceutical chemical compounds increasingly found in the environment, their adverse effects to humans and other living species, and specifically to the threat associated with the antibiotic resistance phenomenon and the management thereof.

children.name: Employee transportation
children.description: Refers to schemes that support employee commuting and traveling. This topic also includes initiatives that avoid or reduce unnecessary travel while encouraging more sustainable practices

children.name: Energy use, conservation & reductions
children.description: Refers to the consumption of fuel and electricity, and overall energy use by companies, as well as energy recycling, energy efficiency gains and overall reduction measures. Energy companies will find references to meeting energy demand while those involved in electricity T&D will find references to distribution systems included in this topic

children.name: Fair & equitable compensation
children.description: Refers to the principles, measures and initiatives to offer fair, transparent and consistent employee compensation without favor or prejudice, and ensure it meets minimum liveable standards given the local cost of living.

children.name: GHG emissions & reductions
children.description: Refers to the direct and indirect emissions of greenhouse gases (GHGs) and emission reduction targets aimed at limiting individual company contributions to global warming.

children.name: Hazardous waste management
children.description: Refers to operational waste that poses a threat to human health or the environment, and the waste management practices to dispose of it responsibly.

children.name: Managing land use & reducing deforestation
children.description: Refers to the use and management of the Earth's land surface, soil and forest resources to meet business and customer needs, as well as measures of any relevant contamination, deterioration or destruction. It covers environmentally responsible practices designed to maintain and improve the Earth's long-term biodiversity, regeneration capacity and productivity.

children.name: Non-hazardous waste management
children.description: Refers to the management, disposal and volume of innocuous operational waste that has not been recovered for recycling or reuse.

children.name: Outer space management
children.description: Refers to the processes and systems to control and regulate human activities in outer space. It includes the management of space traffic and low earth orbit, the mitigation of space debris, the prevention of collisions, and the minimization of the impacts of space weather.

children.name: Physical disasters & failures
children.description: Refers to catastrophic events resulting from natural processes or human error and negligence, including significant failure of critical public or private utility systems and assets.

children.name: Plastics management
children.description: Refers to the negative aspects of plastic pollution with the potential to harm wildlife and humans, as well as measures to mitigate those - including the collection, segregation, and recycling of all kinds of plastics to move towards a circular economy - and initiatives to reduce or eliminate plastic use.

children.name: Project closure, decommissioning & environmental restoration
children.description: Refers to the activities of the final stage in the life-cycle of facilities that are no longer suitable for operations, including plant decommissioning, deconstruction, land reclamation, and management of other environmental liabilities.

children.name: Protection of natural land cover
children.description: Refers to the sustainable management, conservation and restoration of the Earth's land surface, soil, forest and wetland resources, including sustainable agriculture practices.

children.name: Recycling, waste recovery & reduction
children.description: Refers to measures to reduce the amount of waste produced and sent for final disposal, efforts to recover waste for useful material harvesting and reuse, and recycling.

children.name: Smarter & greener transportation
children.description: Refers to more efficient, eco-friendly modes of transportation and logistics management, as well as smarter transportation systems and infrastructure planning.

children.name: Spills
children.description: Refers to leaks, spills and discharges of substances that have negative environmental or human health impacts. It includes corporate preparedness, response plans, clean-up and remediation activities.

children.name: Sustainable building management
children.description: Refers to reducing the environmental footprint of buildings and facilities across their life-cycles, from design to construction, operation, maintenance, renovation, and demolition. References to interior planning and design considerations, like more natural light and plants, to improve the indoor environmental quality are also included in this topic.

children.name: Transition to a circular economy
children.description: Refers to principles, practices and processes that minimize or eliminate the negative environmental impacts of a product across its lifecycle in an effort to eliminate waste and reduce virgin input materials. Aspects of producer responsibility for their products are included in this topic, as are industry-specific models to reduce the environmental footprint of products and services.

children.name: Transition to renewable energy
children.description: Refers to the transition from a predominantly fossil-based energy production and consumption system to zero-carbon and renewable energy sources, including policies, goals, accounting instruments, services and technologies that facilitate that transition.

children.name: Water
children.description: Refers to the use, management and conservation of water resources for meeting business and customer needs. It includes the availability of freshwater, the efforts to reduce water consumption, and the treatment and re-use of wastewater and greywater.

children.name: Water pollution
children.description: Refers to the human-induced contamination of bodies of water, affecting the water quality, directly or indirectly harming other living species, or posing a hazard to human health.

children.name: Access to health care & medicine
children.description: Refers to initiatives aimed at providing equal access to comprehensive, quality and timely health care services for all people. It covers activities aimed at expanding access to medicine and treatments for more people, as well as research projects on unmet medical needs

children.name: Animal welfare
children.description: Refers to consideration of the physical and mental welfare of animals that are central to some business models, including practices aimed at maintaining an environment in which captive animals can live and develop as they would have in their natural habitats

children.name: Biotechnology & ethical considerations
children.description: Refers to innovations and breakthroughs that use biology to improve human, animal, and plant life by manipulating their genetic structures and biomolecular processes. It includes mentions of medicines and therapeutics that prevent and treat disease, medical diagnostics, genetically modified organisms (GMOs), and cultured food. It also incorporates ethical concerns related to these technologies.

children.name: Changing consumption patterns
children.description: Refers to both sides of the producer-consumer paradigm: producers educating consumers on social and environmental issues and consumer activism to shift production patterns and products towards increased sustainability. General trends oriented around sustainable production & consumption are also included in this topic.

children.name: Children's rights
children.description: Refers to the protection of the rights of the child as relates to anticipating, preventing or responding to any form of violence, exploitation, abuse or practices that might be harmful to a child's physical and mental development and integrity.

children.name: Community engagement
children.description: Refers to the relationship that exists between a company and the communities it operates in and engagement with those communities for consultation or feedback

children.name: Community support & development
children.description: Refers to the business practices and projects carried out to ensure the well-being and positive development of communities with which a company interacts. It covers company-led initiatives aimed at growing a community's ability to develop and implement solutions for improved management of their physical, social, economic and cultural environments

children.name: Consumer nutrition & wellbeing
children.description: Refers to different aspects that relate to consumer nutrition: (1) Food products that provide varied, balanced, or special diets, and the information regarding their nutritional components; (2) Measures to ensure food safety and quality standards; (3) Nutrition for children and adolescents; and (4) References to the effects of food on human health, including medical conditions or chronic diseases.

children.name: Consumer rights
children.description: Refers to consumers' rights to be safe, informed, have choice, and provide feedback to the producers of the goods and services they purchase. It includes mechanisms to ensure consumers are treated fairly and honestly during commercial transactions

children.name: Customer satisfaction
children.description: Refers to the strategies and processes intended to meet or surpass customer expectations and the requirements to create a positive experience and build customer loyalty from the point of purchase onwards

children.name: Employee development
children.description: Refers to the practices, initiatives and processes aimed at nurturing talent and supporting employees in developing the skills necessary to succeed in their next role. Aspects of career progression, including vertical and lateral job moves based on performance assessments, as well as succession planning, are included in this topic

children.name: Employee engagement & satisfaction
children.description: Refers to engagement activities centered on enhancing collaboration and communication between employees and improving their work contributions. It also includes mechanisms to grow employee contentment, accomplishment, satisfaction, and motivation regarding their jobs, and associated satisfaction measurements and metrics

children.name: Employee incentives & benefits
children.description: Refers to financial and non-financial benefits provided to employees, and corporate strategies to leverage those benefits to incentivize performance and employee retention. Due to differing legal obligations by jurisdiction, any benefit provided by the employer above-and-beyond basic pay-for-work compensation, and that is not otherwise enshrined in international labor law, is included in this topic

children.name: Employee wellbeing
children.description: Refers to the mental and physical condition of employees with respect to their health, happiness, comfort and morale. It includes initiatives that promote healthy lifestyles, offer physical and social activities within a company, and support mental health, as well as work arrangements that allow employees a level of autonomy to follow a work schedule aligned with a work-life balance that best suits them

children.name: Epidemics & their multi-dimensional impacts
children.description: Refers to the multidimensional consequences and the collateral impacts that an epidemic brings to society and businesses, including adaptation and mitigation measures.

children.name: Fair & inclusive workplace
children.description: Refers to the processes, practices and mechanisms in place to support the active integration and fair treatment of all employees, with a focus on gender, age, cultural background, skills, race, religion, and sexual orientation. It considers equal opportunity provisions aimed at promoting the professional development of all employees and supporting a culture of acceptance.

children.name: Financial access, literacy & advice
children.description: Refers to practices, processes, and initiatives to ensure that individuals, communities, and businesses have appropriate access to and information on financial services, products, and resources. Financial empowerment of employees and customers through sound advice is also included in this topic.

children.name: Future of work
children.description: Refers to the impacts that digitization, globalization, demographic changes, and Covid-19 have on the jobs and how jobs are and will be performed. It includes the critical skills and requirements that the current and future workforce must meet to adapt and thrive, as well as the strategic management of flexible work arrangements.

children.name: Building skills for a digital world
Transformation of jobs due to automation
Grievance mechanisms & remediation
children.description: Refers to systems and channels aimed at collecting, processing and addressing formal complaints from employees or other parties engaged in business, legal, or societal relationships, including whistleblowing procedures.

children.name: Holistic & patient-centric approach to health
children.description: Refers to practices and services aimed at improving the safety and wellbeing of people, promoting healthy lifestyles, preventing diseases, and educating patients, caregivers and the general public on health issues. It covers personalized support provided to patients, as well as the proper use of medication and available treatments.

children.name: Human rights
children.description: Refers to the fundamental rights and freedoms inherent to all human beings that ensure they are able to live with dignity, freedom, equality, justice, and peace, and the measures necessary to uphold these rights.

children.name: Inclusive products & services
children.description: Refers to products and services that integrate characteristics to make them accessible and usable by as many people as reasonably possible, with particular focus on low-income individuals or groups, entrepreneurs or small businesses, and people with disabilities.

children.name: Indigenous populations
children.description: Refers to communities that live within geographically distinct traditional habitats or ancestral territories, and who identify themselves as being part of a distinct cultural group. It includes engagement with indigenous people in reference to the land, workplace and societal contexts.

children.name: Labor rights
children.description: Refers to the legal rights and fundamental principles that regulate labor relations between workers and employers, including stipulations of employment and working conditions.

children.name: Local economy support
children.description: Refers to the production, distribution, and procurement activities that use local resources to stimulate economic growth and development while generating social, commercial, and working opportunities for residents.

children.name: Market access
children.description: Refers to the mechanisms that guarantee an open and free-market economy as well as to government policies that restrict international trade for the benefit of a domestic economy, including tariffs, subsidies, import quotas, product standards, currency manipulation and commercial or financial penalties.

children.name: Marketing & selling practices
children.description: Refers to both responsible and unethical advertisements, communications and sales strategies, with a focus on the honest representation of the characteristics, impacts, and safety of products and services.

children.name: Occupational health & safety
children.description: Refers to health and safety in the workplace with a focus on primary risk prevention. On the one hand, it integrates protocols, training and the design of optimal conditions for employee productivity, while on the other hand it refers to work-related injuries, illnesses and death, as well as behaviors and workplace risks that can cause physical, psychological, sexual or economic damage to employees.

children.name: Philanthropy & volunteering
children.description: Refers to the contribution to other organizations and communities through funds, volunteering efforts, and in-kind products or services. It also includes assistance given to people in immediate distress during and after emergencies and disasters.

children.name: Product & service safety & quality
children.description: Refers to the initiatives, procedures and staff training to ensure product and service quality and the safety of end consumers, including considerations regarding product ingredients and their potential effects on customer health. References to breaches of product or service quality and safety and general liability concerns are included in this topic.

children.name: Public health
children.description: Refers to the major contributors to disease in humans, as well as factors that diminish a person's ability to live a fulfilling life.

children.name: Shifting demographics
children.description: Refers to the impact that any localized or global demographic trends may have on the economic, political and social spheres. It includes services and products designed to address the problems or needs that arise from these changes, which are based on differing factors such as birth or death rates, age structure, migration, education and income.

children.name: Social inclusion
children.description: Refers to programs and initiatives addressing key social issues of marginalization, exclusion, inequality and poverty, and general themes focused on ensuring all people are able to participate in modern society.

children.name: Socio-economic risks
children.description: Refers to societal and macroeconomic issues that may pose risks to business if not handled appropriately by national or subnational governments or agencies.

children.name: Unionization
children.description: Refers to the relationship between labor unions and employers and related matters, including collective bargaining.

children.name: Workforce & organizational change management
children.description: Refers to strategic decisions and general trends involving workforce changes, as well as the structures, procedures and technologies in place to manage that change and successfully implement new processes and commercial strategies while minimizing negative impacts.

children.name: Advanced technologies & innovations
children.description: Refers to developing or leveraging emerging technologies and digital innovations that transform business operations and how people live. References to the opportunities and risks presented by these technologies and innovations are included in this topic.

children.name: Artificial intelligence
children.description: Refers to computer programs that respond to stimulation, analyze data, and use the resulting insights to make decisions in a way that is consistent with human intelligence and adaptation. It involves new use cases and application ideas, as well as references to concerns about ethical considerations

children.name: Board effectiveness
children.description: Refers to processes aimed at ensuring and evaluating the knowledge, skills and experience of board members, as well as related performance standards. It covers the structure and composition of a board, including diversity, as well its guiding principles and continuity & improvement practices

children.name: Business continuity
children.description: Refers to the plans, actions, protocols and training conducted to ensure continuity of core business operations during and after crises that may disrupt commercial activities. It also covers strategies to adapt business models to actual or potential changes in the external environment.

children.name: Business ethics
children.description: Refers to the guidelines or codes that dictate fair, ethical and moral executive and employee conduct, as well as training on the subject and implementation by staff and management. It also covers ethical standards at the leadership level in decision-making processes.

children.name: Business model innovation
children.description: Refers to new business models realizable through technological advances and increased digital access that provide innovative approaches to redefining traditional service offerings

children.name: Certification, labeling & information transparency
children.description: Refers to the information that companies share with consumers so that they can make informed decisions about the goods and services they purchase, as well as the relevant contractual terms & conditions and applicable rates, for industries where applicable. It also covers independent third-party certifications that verify environmental or social aspects of the products, and associated labeling

children.name: Climate change risks & management
children.description: Refers to the physical and transition impacts, risks and opportunities presented by climate change and the transition to a low-carbon economy, as well as adaptation and resilience measures adopted by companies. References to governance systems to manage climate-related risks and opportunities, as well as social considerations related to climate justice and just transition, are also included in this topic.

children.name: Compliance management
children.description: Refers to the mechanisms that ensure a company's awareness of and compliance with relevant laws, policies, and regulations. It also covers processes to identify and proactively address risks of litigation or regulatory action, and prevent disputes from arising or escalating.

children.name: Corporate criminal liability
children.description: Refers to the cases in which a legal person is criminally responsible for a criminal offense and the preventative corporate policies, procedures, and processes designed to meet legal compliance obligations and mitigate corruption risks. References to criminal investigations, legal proceedings, and penalties for misconduct are also included in this topic.

children.name: Corporate culture
children.description: Refers to the fundamental beliefs and guiding principles that dictate behavior within a company and determine how the company's employees and leaders interact and handle business transactions with all stakeholders

children.name: Corporate reputation
children.description: Refers to how a company presents itself to the outside world regarding performance, particularly on sustainability issues, and strategic decisions. It covers the positive and negative perceptions of stakeholders and associated risks based on the company’s past actions and its perceived ability to deliver value

children.name: Cybersecurity & information security
children.description: Refers to the breakdown or vulnerability of critical information systems and networks due to security breaches, which might disrupt core operations or lead to accidental or illegal access, destruction, alteration or disclosure of protected data. It covers the mechanisms for protecting computers, networks, programs and data, as well as remediation measures if breaches occur

children.name: Data privacy management
children.description: Refers to the collection, storage, processing, usage and sharing of data that is, or might be, connected to an identifiable person, including sensitive personal information. It covers mandatory and voluntary regulations and security mechanisms that aim to protect personal data, as well as instances of violation of individuals' privacy

children.name: Law enforcement requests for personal data
Digital transformation
children.description: Refers to the integration and increased use of digital technologies to modify or produce new business processes and improve the experiences of customers and other stakeholders. It alludes to the different cultural environments that embrace or restrict digital innovation, as well as the equal and unrestricted access to internet data and its traffic.

children.name: Employee acquisition, talent management & retention
children.description: Refers to the mechanisms used to find, recruit, manage and retain the right employees and talent to build a skilled workforce and boost business growth, especially in an environment of increasing competition for skills. It also covers the strategic management of outsourced teams, contractors, freelancers or gig workers, as well as efforts to grow employee loyalty and successfully retain staff

children.name: Competition for talent & building a skilled workforce
ESG Governance structure
children.description: Refers to the governance bodies and organizational structures that define and manage an organization’s ESG strategy, objectives and goals

children.name: Ethics of clinical trials
children.description: Refers to ethical and inclusive practices employed when planning and conducting clinical trials. It covers patient involvement, safety, informed consent, incentives to participants, high clinical/laboratory practice standards, as well as diverse representation of those affected by the disease or condition treated.

children.name: Executive compensation
children.description: Refers to financial and non-financial compensation provided to senior management, executives and board members

children.name: Fair competition
children.description: Refers to business and government mechanisms aimed at maintaining healthy marketplaces where all participants compete fairly on a level playing field. It also includes practices that harm the general interests of competition in a corresponding market, by preventing or reducing it.

children.name: Geopolitical risks
children.description: Refers to political and military pressures that may pose risks to business if not handled appropriately by national or subnational governments or agencies.

children.name: Harmful substances management
children.description: Refers to substances and materials used in an organization's operations or present in its products that pose a threat to human health or the environment. References to specific controversial ingredients included in products can be found in the Product & service safety & quality topic.

children.name: Innovation management
children.description: Refers to processes, initiatives and activities aimed at the advancement of products and services through various approaches, including open innovation, crowdsourcing and the use of new technologies. Go-to-market strategies and measures to protect a competitive edge and increase market penetration are also included in this topic.

children.name: Intellectual property
children.description: Refers to both the protection of or infringement on exclusive rights given to an individual or legal entity for an idea, invention or creative expression that acquires the status of 'property'. It includes patents, trademarks, copyrights, and trade secrets.

children.name: Investor relations
children.description: Refers to relationship management and engagement practices between companies and their shareholders, current and potential investors, and financial analysts. The rights and practices of shareholders, including proxy voting, are also included in this topic.

children.name: Long-term value creation
children.description: Refers to a company's processes and practices aimed at optimizing its financial, social and environmental value in the long term.

children.name: Materials management
children.description: Refers to the management of materials, recognizing the reality of resource scarcity where measured usage, increased recycled inputs and reduced virgin resources can contribute to improved environmental conditions. It includes a specific focus on forest products, metals & minerals and plastics, which have wide-reaching value chain impacts as source materials.

children.name: Non-financial reporting
children.description: Refers to the processes and standards for the accounting, reporting and auditing of non-financial information, including its integration with financial disclosures. References to the actors & ecosystems that support non-financial transparency are also included in this topic.

children.name: Online safety
children.description: Refers to the threats that exist on the Internet and the negative consequences that affect humans due to the use of new technologies.

children.name: Project-based social & environmental impact assessment
children.description: Refers to the assessments and processes to predict, manage and mitigate potential negative social and environmental impacts of business projects, including addressing community resettlements where unavoidable.

children.name: Public policy practices
children.description: Refers to the the relationship between companies and governments/ officials and the practices adopted by companies to influence public policy, including lobbying and political contributions. It also includes considerations of the alignment of overt and covert policy positions with corporate values.

children.name: Responsible investing & financing
children.description: Refers to investment products aimed at producing a specific sustainability-oriented outcome, the financing of projects or companies with an environmental or social benefit, and investing and financing practices specifically geared to making a positive impact.

children.name: Responsible pricing
children.description: Refers to corporate policies and practices to ensure the pricing of its products is fair, transparent, and non-discriminatory.

children.name: Responsible procurement
children.description: Refers to the policies and practices to procure ethically and responsibly produced goods or services for a fair price.

children.name: Responsible tax practices
children.description: Refers to the taxation of a company's profits and related matters, including state incentives and corporate strategies to reduce the amount of taxes levied or paid, the effective exchange of information with tax authorities, and illegal activities aimed at evading the imposition or payment of a tax.

children.name: Solvency & financial management
children.description: Refers to the regulatory requirements and measures of a bank or other financial institution’s financial strengths and solvency, including stress tests and capital buffers, among others.

children.name: Stakeholder engagement
children.description: Refers to the process of organizing, monitoring, maintaining and improving relationships and communication streams with people or groups who may affect or be affected by a company's activities and decisions.

children.name: Supply chain management
children.description: Refers to the policies, practices and goals established by a company that dictate how it chooses suppliers, measures supplier performance and alignment with core and strategic values, audits suppliers, engages with and supports suppliers to improve on ESG issues, and maintains transparency from cradle to gate to ensure sourcing commitments are met.

children.name: Supply chain policies, screening, auditing & corrective actions

relationships.source: Drug resistance & pharmaceuticals in the environment
Drug resistance & pharmaceuticals in the environment
Drug resistance & pharmaceuticals in the environment
